description_long: This is a modern fireman's axe featuring a robust, slightly curved handle with a textured black grip and a bright red shaft. The head of the axe is large and heavy, with a wide, flat blade on one side and a pointed pick on the opposite side, both finished in a metallic gray. The blade edge is straight and sharp, while the pick end is tapered for piercing. The handle appears to be made of a durable composite material, likely fiberglass or reinforced plastic, designed for impact resistance and ergonomic handling. The color scheme of red and black is typical for emergency tools, enhancing visibility in urban environments. No visible markings, engravings, or brand labels are present on the axe.
description: A modern fireman's axe with a red and black composite handle and a heavy, dual-ended metallic head featuring a blade and a pick.
description_view_0: The axe is shown from a side angle, highlighting the curved handle, red shaft, and the full profile of the blade and pick.
description_view_1: This view displays the axe from the front, emphasizing the width of the blade and the alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: The axe is seen from the opposite side, showing the curvature of the handle and the pick end of the head.
description_view_3: A top-down perspective reveals the thickness of the axe head and the curvature of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, ergonomic handle made of black and red composite material with a textured, grippy surface and oval cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Single wide blade with a straight edge on one side and a pointed pick on the opposite side; no beard, with a flat poll.
attribute_mounting_style: Modern composite molded mounting with the head integrated into the handle.
